name: Web UI auth middleware (gate dashboard routes)
description: Apply the session-cookie check from web-ui-auth-backend to the endpoints the dashboard uses (routes, trips, signals, storage, settings). Device-facing endpoints (pilotauth, upload, /ws/) must keep using their existing JWT auth -- do not conflate the two. Endpoints that accept either cookie or JWT (for shared read endpoints) should explicitly try both.
acceptance_criteria: internal/api/middleware/session.go adds SessionRequired() echo.MiddlewareFunc that validates the signed session cookie; 401 when missing/invalid
internal/api/middleware/session.go adds SessionOrJWT() echo.MiddlewareFunc that accepts either a valid session cookie OR a valid JWT (tries cookie first, falls back to existing JWT middleware logic)

Route groups in internal/api/ are updated so: /v1/session/* is public; /v1/route/*, /v1/routes/*, /v1/devices/:id/stats, /v1/devices/:id/params (GET), /v1/storage/*, /v1/settings/*, /v1/trips/* use SessionOrJWT; /v1/devices/:id/params (PUT/DELETE) and /v1/settings/* mutations require a session (humans, not devices)
Device-facing endpoints (/v2/pilotauth/, /v1.4/upload_url/, /upload/, /ws/v2/) continue using device JWT only
When SESSION_SECRET is unset (UI auth disabled), SessionRequired/SessionOrJWT degrade to allowing all (open mode); server logs a single warning on startup explaining the state
Middleware test coverage in internal/api/middleware/ for: valid session, expired session, tampered cookie, JWT fallback for SessionOrJWT, open-mode pass-through
go vet ./... and go build ./... pass cleanly
